babyDevelopment: The baby's brain is continuing its final growth spurt. Most physical development is now complete, and the focus is on gaining weight. The kidneys and liver are fully functional. This is a common time for Group B Strep screening.
bodyChanges: You likely have a prenatal check-up every week now. Many women experience 'leg cramps' and 'swelling' reaching a peak. You might feel more anxious about the upcoming birth. Braxton Hicks may feel more intense.
tips: Schedule your Group B Strep (GBS) screening test
Practice pelvic floor exercises (Kegels) to prepare for birth
Elevate your legs at the end of each day to reduce swelling

do: Eat smaller, frequent meals for better digestion
Focus on deep breathing during activities
dont: Eat heavy, fatty foods that trigger heartburn
Ignore signs of severe, persistent swelling
Over-exert yourself as you fatigue easily

travelAdvisory.note: Avoid all non-essential travel; stay close to home.

warningSignsToWatch: Signs of Pre-eclampsia (Sudden severe swelling, headache, vision changes)
Positive Group B Strep results (consult doctor)
milestoneDescription: Baby's primary focus now is gaining weight for energy and warmth after birth.
